source_pr.title: *: support foreign key check/cascade when execute replace into statement
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #40068

Problem Summary: Support foreign key check/cascade when execute replace into statement

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: No explicit flaky failure signature is documented; the PR adds support for foreign key check/cascade when executing `REPLACE INTO`.
failure_signature: Bug fix + regression test (FK cascade/check with `REPLACE INTO`) without a concrete flaky CI failure signature.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch refactors FK trigger handling so `REPLACE INTO` also runs the appropriate foreign key checks/cascades, and adds a regression test. The PR does not provide CI logs or evidence of an intermittent flake (vs. a deterministic correctness bug), so we conservatively avoid guessing a flakiness root cause category.
fix_pattern: Support FK checks/cascades for `REPLACE INTO` by wiring delete-path FK triggers into replace execution; add a regression test.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select id, a, b from t1 order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1 1")) (executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go)

analysis:

PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/40069

Triage summary: tk.MustQuery("select id, a, b from t1 order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1 1")) (executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go)
Changed files (4): executor/delete.go, executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go, executor/replace.go, planner/core/foreign_key.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/40069.patch
Picked test: type=unit path=executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go name=foreign_key_test
